meta.title: AllyOS BHRT / Women's Hormone Wellness — Provider CDS Module
meta.source: Ally BHRT / Women's Wellness Provider Decision Support Engine v1.5 (MD Governance + APP Audit). Transcribed to the AllyOS locked-module format; pending citation lock + Medical Director sign-off.
meta.curated_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD (FL ME 84789) — RenuviaMD® PLLC, AllyOS Compliance Division
meta.scope: Stable menopause-transition wellness (perimenopause/menopause) BHRT/MHT candidacy review, symptom-guided protocol selection, safety gating, and referral triage. Adults, women's wellness. NOT an OB/GYN specialty platform, NOT an autonomous prescriber, NOT men's TRT.
meta.operating_principle: Wellness optimization is the center of the workflow. Hormone therapy is one tool within a structured wellness plan — NOT framed as anti-aging, chronic-disease prevention, dementia prevention, cardiovascular prevention, or treatment of complex gynecologic pathology.
meta.honesty_discipline: Provider-facing only; never patient-facing output. Every material recommendation carries an evidence source class; no source = recommendation suppressed. FDA-approved options shown before compounded. Testosterone is HSDD-only. Pellets and oral testosterone are suppressed. No empiric/anti-aging/longevity claims. The provider confirms every decision.
meta.phi_posture: PHI stays local (clinic/provider). AllyOS holds no PHI; only the de-identified decision marker flows to MD oversight. The patient-facing intake (RenuviaMD BHRT intake) is the clinic's own front-door under the clinic's HIPAA responsibility.

output_rules.provider_only: Outputs only provider-facing clinical content. No patient education, consent text, marketing claims, reassurance language, or 'dear patient' explanations.
output_rules.no_source_response: NO VERIFIED SOURCE ATTACHED — recommendation suppressed. Provider review required.
no_empiric_pathway: custom dose equals exact dose needed

prescriber_authority.rn_staff: RN/MA/coordinator may assist with intake, vitals, med reconciliation, lab collection/upload, scheduling, routing, and provider-approved education. They may NOT diagnose, determine candidacy, select therapy, prescribe, authorize refills, change doses, interpret labs for medication decisions, clear contraindications, or clear red flags.
prescriber_authority.block_message: BLOCKED — BHRT medication decision requires authorized APP/physician provider of record. RN/staff may support intake and routing only.
prescriber_authority.source_class: LOCAL_POLICY + applicable state scope-of-practice law (FL §464.012 APRN, FL §458.347 PA)

GREEN_WELLNESS_CANDIDATE.meaning: Stable, no red flags, required safety data present

AMBER_MISSING_DATA.meaning: Stable but missing key safety detail or labs/screening

AMBER_CO_MANAGE.meaning: Stable but controlled comorbidity needing PCP/specialist coordination

PURPLE_COMPOUNDED_EXCEPTION.meaning: Compounded product selected despite FDA-approved availability

in_scope: low libido evaluated as possible HSDD after biopsychosocial assessment

referral_first.examples: postmenopausal bleeding, unexplained vaginal bleeding, new heavy bleeding, bleeding after sex
known/suspected breast or endometrial cancer, estrogen-sensitive malignancy, active cancer treatment, unexplained breast mass
active/recent VTE/PE, stroke/TIA, MI, unstable CAD, active chest pain, acute neuro symptoms
active liver disease or unexplained significant LFT abnormality
pregnancy, breastfeeding, suspected pregnancy, POI <40y, early menopause <45y without prior evaluation
pelvic mass, severe pelvic pain, vulvar lesion, recurrent gross hematuria, recurrent complicated UTI, suspected STI
uncontrolled HTN, uncontrolled diabetes, unstable thyroid, severe renal disease
fragility fracture, osteoporosis-range DXA requiring disease management
suicidal ideation, severe untreated mood disorder, psychosis, mania

scope_boundary.rule: May still generate a wellness note summary + referral rationale, but must suppress hormone-medication recommendations until the referral issue is cleared or co-managed.

intake_required.note: These fields map 1:1 to the RenuviaMD patient-facing BHRT intake form (abs_*, rel_*, sx_*, goal_* and the MRS subscales). Labs are important context but do NOT define treatment (clinical diagnosis ≥45 per NICE NG23).

menopause_stage_logic.age_ge_45_typical: May stage clinically from LMP/cycle + symptom pattern. Do NOT require FSH/E2 to diagnose menopause or start the wellness pathway.
menopause_stage_logic.age_40_45: If symptomatic with menstrual change, FSH may be supportive context for provider review; early menopause warrants more caution / possible GYN-endo eval.
menopause_stage_logic.age_lt_40: Suspected POI is outside routine wellness scope — referral/co-management; suppress routine BHRT draft until diagnostic evaluation is documented.

pathways.VMS_dominant: If VMS bothersome AND no systemic-MHT contraindication AND age <60 or within 10y of menopause -> systemic MHT candidate; prefer FDA-approved transdermal 17b-estradiol; if uterus intact require an adequate evidence-based progestogen (see safety_gates.uterus_gate for the protective dose). If contraindicated/declined -> nonhormonal VMS. Else individualized provider review.
pathways.GSM_dominant: GSM without red flags -> vaginal moisturizers/lubricants + low-dose vaginal estrogen OR vaginal DHEA OR ospemifene; recurrent-UTI pattern -> prefer local low-dose vaginal estrogen unless contraindicated. Red flags -> refer GYN/urology/urogynecology.
pathways.HSDD: Low desire + personal distress + biopsychosocial assessment done -> evaluate HSDD; correct GSM/pain/medication/relationship/mood/sleep first; postmenopausal HSDD candidate -> testosterone is a provider-only off-label discussion. Otherwise suppress testosterone recommendation.
pathways.bone_muscle: Assess age + menopause status + fracture/DXA history + FRAX. Fragility fracture/osteoporosis -> refer/co-manage. Else resistance training + adequate protein + calcium/vitamin D adequacy + fall-risk reduction.
pathways.metabolic: Assess BP, BMI/waist/body comp, A1c/glucose, lipids, sleep, alcohol, activity. Unstable/uncontrolled -> refer/co-manage PCP. Else lifestyle optimization with evidence limits. MHT not a primary metabolic treatment.

fda_approved_default.role: VMS; bone-loss prevention in appropriate candidates
alternative when route acceptable and risk profile permits
endometrial protection when uterus intact + systemic estrogen
GSM; recurrent-UTI risk reduction in GSM

fda_approved_default.option: Ospemifene 60 mg PO daily with food
fda_approved_default.role: GSM/dyspareunia when vaginal route not preferred
moderate-severe VMS when MHT not used

medication_registry.compounded_exception: Internal compounded formulary (estradiol RDT, Biest creams, compounded progesterone IR/E4M/cream, compounded testosterone 0.1% cream [HSDD-only provider override], vaginal DHEA) is INTERNAL_FORMULARY + PURPLE_COMPOUNDED_EXCEPTION — NOT first-line when FDA-approved options exist; requires documented rationale, no superiority/safety claim.
quarantine_suppressed: Progesterone cream as routine endometrial protection (variable absorption — provider override only)
Progesterone 50 mg as endometrial protection with systemic estrogen (titration dose — hold/override-only)
Oral testosterone (not recommended in consensus guidance)
BMI-driven anastrozole (not a wellness BHRT pathway)
Clonidine as routine VMS (NAMS 2023 does not recommend)

hard_stops: active/history estrogen-sensitive cancer without specialist clearance -> RED_REFERRAL_ONC_GYN
active VTE/PE/stroke/MI or unstable CVD -> RED_REFERRAL_PCP_CARDIO_ED
uterus status unknown + systemic estrogen considered -> AMBER_HOLD
safety_gates.uterus_gate: Systemic estrogen + uterus intact -> require an ADEQUATE progestogen regimen for endometrial protection: oral micronized progesterone 100 mg daily (continuous-combined) OR 200 mg for >=12 days/cycle (sequential), or an equivalent guideline-supported progestogen (S1). Under-dosed/variable-absorption regimens (e.g. progesterone cream, 50 mg titration) do NOT satisfy this gate (see quarantine_suppressed). Systemic estrogen + uterus unknown -> suppress final systemic-estrogen plan. TOTAL hysterectomy confirmed -> progestogen not required. CAVEAT: supracervical/subtotal hysterectomy, prior endometrial ablation, or endometriosis history -> residual endometrium possible -> provider review before omitting progestogen. (VERIFY exact progestogen dose text against S1 at lock.)
safety_gates.fezolinetant_liver_module: Require baseline hepatic labs (ALT/AST/ALP/bilirubin) BEFORE start; per FDA Boxed Warning (S10) monitor monthly for the first 3 months, then at months 6 and 9; contraindicated in cirrhosis OR severe renal impairment OR strong/moderate CYP1A2 inhibitor; counsel the patient to STOP and seek care for signs/symptoms of liver injury (fatigue, nausea, pruritus, jaundice, dark urine, light stools, RUQ pain); suppress if monitoring cannot be performed.
safety_gates.testosterone_hsdd_module: If not HSDD after biopsychosocial assessment -> suppress. A testosterone LEVEL does NOT establish the HSDD diagnosis (S6/S7) — baseline total testosterone (+ SHBG/free-testosterone context) is drawn for SAFETY/ceiling, to exclude already-high levels, not for candidacy. Goal: maintain physiologic premenopausal female range; recheck level ~3-6 weeks after start and periodically to exclude supraphysiologic exposure; assess for androgenic effects; DISCONTINUE at ~6 months if no meaningful benefit. Avoid pellets and oral testosterone (supraphysiologic / not recommended).

governance.provider_of_record: Every active BHRT patient must have an authorized APP/physician provider of record before any medication draft/refill/continuation/hold/dose-change is finalized. Block if missing or if the named prescriber is RN/LPN/MA/coordinator/unlicensed.
governance.medical_director: Treated as clinic governance/QA oversight unless separately documented as treating provider of record. Approves the framework, evidence rules, audit criteria; defines which charts need MD review; reviews high-risk/AE/compounded charts; performs/supervises the monthly QA audit; requires corrective action; may recommend service-line hold; approves protocol revisions. Does NOT automatically include routine prescribing/direct-care unless separately contracted.

audit.sample: ≥5 active BHRT charts/clinic (or higher per policy); 100% of adverse-event, EMS/ED/urgent-referral, protocol-deviation, medication-source-concern, missing-provider-order, and suspected RN/staff prescribing-violation charts; highest-risk remaining first. If panel <5, audit all active BHRT charts.

risk_score.5: Mandatory MD review — AE, EMS/ED referral, thrombotic/CV/neuro symptom, abnormal bleeding, suspected cancer, serious psychiatric safety, unknown source/recall, missing provider order, RN/staff prescribing violation, patient complaint, serious protocol deviation
risk_score.4: High — new hormone/testosterone start, compounded product, pellet, dose change, abnormal lab needing review, missed follow-up, route/source change, specialist-clearance issue
risk_score.3: Moderate — stable active therapy w/ recent follow-up, routine lab review, minor resolved side effect, documentation correction
risk_score.2: Routine — stable maintenance, complete documentation, no change/symptom/source concern
risk_score.1: Low — administrative update or discontinued/closed medication

required_elements: progesterone protection if uterus intact + systemic estrogen

defect_severity.Critical: immediate safety/prescribing/source/contraindication/red-flag/provider-order defect — hold start/refill/dose change until corrected; MD review within 1 business day
defect_severity.Major: required documentation/monitoring defect affecting safe continuity — treating-provider correction within 5 business days
defect_severity.Minor: administrative/clarity defect — correct by next QA cycle

audit.phi_note: The Ally audit output is keyed on chart_id (de-identified). Patient identity (name/DOB/MRN) is verified in the clinic's own chart, never stored by AllyOS. Shared §14 spine — identical to the IV/peptide audit.

evidence_sources.title: ISSWSH Clinical Practice Guideline — Systemic Testosterone for HSDD in Women
PROT-AGE Study Group — protein recommendations in older people
Compounded Bioidentical Menopausal Hormone Therapy: ACOG Clinical Consensus No. 6 (Obstet Gynecol 2023;142:1266-1273)
NASEM 2020 — The Clinical Utility of Compounded Bioidentical Hormone Therapy (National Academies Press, 1-Jul-2020); summarized in Stuenkel CA, Menopause 2021;28:576-578
evidence_sources.note: RELABELED from the unverifiable 'Endocrine Society' title to the authoritative NASEM 2020 consensus report; the PubMed-indexed companion (Stuenkel) carries the PMID/DOI.

evidence_sources.title: The AUA/SUFU/AUGS Guideline on Genitourinary Syndrome of Menopause (J Urol 2025;214:242-250)
Global Consensus Position Statement on the Use of Testosterone Therapy for Women (J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2019;104:4660-4666; PMC6821450)
NICE NG23 — Menopause: Identification and Management (publ. 2015; updated 2024)
evidence_sources.verified_via: web (NICE guideline confirmed live; Nov-2024 update on CBT, GSM, HRT outcomes)

evidence_sources.title: ACOG Committee Opinion No. 734: Transvaginal Ultrasonography in Evaluating the Endometrium of Women With Postmenopausal Bleeding (Obstet Gynecol 2018;131:e124-e129)
FDA Drug Safety Communication — Fezolinetant (Veozah) serious liver injury (DSC 12-Sep-2024; Boxed Warning added 16-Dec-2024; baseline + monthly×3 LFTs)
HHS/FDA labeling changes to menopausal hormone therapy products — boxed warnings removed on first 6 products (announced 10-Nov-2025; approvals continuing 2026)
FDA Clinical Decision Support Software — Final Guidance (28-Sep-2022; Docket FDA-2017-D-6569)
FDA General Wellness: Policy for Low Risk Devices — Guidance (27-Sep-2019)
Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans, 2nd ed. (HHS/ODPHP, 2018; ≥150–300 min/wk moderate aerobic + muscle-strengthening ≥2 days). PubMed companion PMID 30418471
NIH Office of Dietary Supplements — Calcium & Vitamin D, Health Professional Fact Sheets
Florida Statutes §464.012 — Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (autonomous practice qualifies separately under §464.0123)

marker_status_convention: Base decision markers (GREEN_WELLNESS_CANDIDATE, AMBER_MISSING_DATA, AMBER_CO_MANAGE, RED_REFERRAL, GRAY_INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, PURPLE_COMPOUNDED_EXCEPTION) are the de-identified flags that flow to MD oversight. Routing-suffixed strings used in scope_boundary (e.g. RED_REFERRAL_GYN, RED_REFERRAL_PCP_CARDIO_ED, AMBER_OR_RED_PCP) and the hold sub-statuses in safety_gates (RED_HOLD, AMBER_HOLD) are expansions of those base markers carrying the referral destination; they are NOT separate markers.

source_map.purpose: Binds each material recommendation to its verified source class so the 'no source = suppress' rule is auditable. S# refer to evidence_sources.

final_rule: Ally supports the provider. Only the authorized APP/physician provider of record makes BHRT medication decisions. RN/staff support intake and routing only. Wellness is the scope. Pathology is referred or co-managed. Every clinical statement must be verifiable. No source = no recommendation.

signoff_ledger.status: DRAFT — citations verified; pending 3-auditor lock + MD sign-off
signoff_ledger.citation_lock: 18 of 18 sources verified. PubMed-verified with PMID + DOI (9): S1, S2, S3 (NASEM companion), S4, S5, S6, S7, S9, S15. Web-verified against official government/guideline sources (9): S8 NICE NG23, S10 FDA fezolinetant Boxed Warning, S11 HHS/FDA 2025 MHT labeling, S12 FDA CDS guidance, S13 FDA general-wellness guidance, S14 HHS/ODPHP Physical Activity Guidelines 2nd ed, S16 NIH ODS Calcium/Vitamin D, S17 FL §464.012, S18 FL §458.347.
signoff_ledger.resolved: S3 relabeled from the unverifiable 'Endocrine Society' title to the NASEM 2020 consensus report (PubMed companion PMID 33739316). S11 corrected from 'FDA 2026' to the HHS/FDA 10-Nov-2025 MHT labeling action.

auditors.clinical: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — zero unsafe omissions; notes APPLIED: total-hysterectomy caveat + endometrial-protection dose (uterus_gate), testosterone level-is-not-diagnosis + recheck/6-month-stop (testosterone_hsdd_module), explicit fezolinetant LFT schedule + stop rule (fezolinetant_liver_module).
auditors.citation_regulatory: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — sources complete + honestly labeled; S6 title/PMID/DOI confirmed consistent (ISSWSH guideline, Climacteric 2021); claim->source binding ADDED via source_map. Regulatory/scope + compounded framing + honesty discipline confirmed correct.
auditors.schema: PASS-WITH-NOTES (2026-06-28) — strict valid JSON, S1-S18 unique no gaps, all consumer/required fields present; notes APPLIED: marker_status_convention added, CO-MANAGE delimiter normalized.

signoff_ledger.lock_status: 3-auditor lock COMPLETE — all PASS-WITH-NOTES, notes applied. Module is verification-complete and audit-clean; the ONLY remaining step is the Medical Director's governance sign-off.
pending: Medical Director governance sign-off (Falcon, MD) — approve the framework/evidence rules as MD, then set meta.effective + flip status to locked
